definitions.definition: The process of reproduction by which a new individual comes into existence from the parent. It involves the fusion of male and female gametes to form a zygote.
A cell resulting from the union of two gametes (sperm and egg) during reproduction.
The process by which a sperm cell fuses with an egg cell to form a zygote.
The process of development of an embryo from a zygote to a fetus.
An early stage of development of a multicellular organism, from the fertilized egg to the end of the embryonic stage.
A late stage of development of a multicellular organism, from the end of the embryonic stage until birth.
The development of a multicellular organism from conception to birth.
The period of time from conception to birth, during which the fertilized egg develops into a fetus.
The medical care provided to a pregnant woman during the prenatal period, which includes regular check-ups, tests, and advice.
The branch of medicine dealing with the care of women during pregnancy, childbirth, and the postpartum period.
The branch of medicine dealing with the health and diseases of the female reproductive system.
The process by which living organisms produce offspring, which can be sexual or asexual.
The reproductive cells of an organism, which can be sperm or egg cells.
The genetic makeup of an individual, which determines the traits and characteristics of the offspring.
The physical and behavioral characteristics of an individual, which result from the interaction of the genotype and the environment.
The passing of traits and characteristics from parents to offspring through the transmission of genetic information.
